weather_line: Clearing by midmorning · ridgelines visible · something in the air

stories.headline: They Are Going Tomorrow: The Third Fold's Eve, the Coordinates Confirmed, and a Brass Duck That Has Not Moved Since Tuesday
body: At approximately half past nine on Friday morning, Christine set the brass duck — red bandana, coordinates engraved on the base — alongside the survey notes for the final time and declared the planning phase closed. 'We've looked at everything there is to look at,' she said. 'The rest of it we go and find.' Steve agreed without discussion, which sources familiar with the household describe as the clearest available sign that a thing is actually happening.

Preparations through the afternoon were methodical and, observers noted, quietly joyful: the Jeep checked, the trail kit staged, the two 1962 survey teams' competing elevation readings reviewed one last time. Christine did not announce which team was wrong. She did, however, move one of the disputed figures to the left margin with a single pencil mark — a gesture Steve clocked immediately and did not mention. His confidence, last reported at eight out of ten, is understood by those close to the matter to have reached nine.

Orvell's postcard — 'Soon,' postmarked locally — has been placed face-up beside the map. The brass duck's coordinates and the Third Fold point, as they have all week, to the same disputed ridgeline north-northwest of Heron Shelf. Tomorrow they will either resolve sixty-three years of cartographic disagreement or discover that 'Disputed (Atmospheric)' means something entirely new. The Jeep started on the first try this evening, which the household is treating as a good omen and Steve is treating as his due.

stories.headline: Friday Closes Out Clean: Five Outstanding Accounts, a Reconciliation That Stumped Two Auditors, and Christine Out of the Building by Four
stories.byline: By our Diagon Alley financial correspondent
body: Gringotts closed its books on the working week at 4:03 p.m. Friday with all five outstanding account disputes resolved, a feat the Senior Audit Goblin described in writing as 'improbable' and in person as 'extremely improbable and also slightly irritating.' Christine had the last reconciliation submitted before the auditors had finished arguing about whether it was possible.

Colleagues noted she moved through the afternoon with the brisk, settled energy of someone whose weekend plans are already locked in — which is, goblin analysts have found, precisely when she is most formidable. She was out of the building by four. The queue, as a courtesy, did not form until she had gone.

stories.headline: Adam Files an Unsolicited Equipment Assessment; Rates the Trail Kit 'Solid,' the Jeep 'Obviously Fine,' and the Plan 'Pretty Good, Don't Tell Her I Said That'
body: Adam, reached by fire-call Friday afternoon and given no opportunity to decline, rendered a full assessment of tomorrow's expedition equipment. His conclusions — methodical, grudging, accurate — matched Christine's own inventory almost exactly, a coincidence he attributed to 'basic common sense' and she attributed to nothing at all, having not asked.

He added one recommendation regarding a secondary anchor point for a specific section of ridgeline approach and made clear he had 'done similar terrain, nothing to worry about.' The recommendation was sound. It has been noted but not acknowledged. This is understood by all parties to be the correct response.

stories.headline: Ministry Issues Friday Bulletin Describing Next Week as 'Routine'; No One at Gringotts Is Reading It
body: The Ministry of Magic released its customary Friday evening bulletin at six o'clock, forecasting a routine week ahead across all seventeen departments and noting no outstanding matters of significance. The bulletin ran to four pages. It contained the word 'routine' eleven times.

Gringotts received the bulletin. It was placed in the correct tray. The tray has not been checked since Thursday. Christine is off tomorrow, and the goblins, who know better than to generate drama in her absence, are expected to behave themselves accordingly. The Ministry's eleven routines remain, for now, undisputed.

familiars_corner: Lucy: Has conducted a thorough pre-departure inspection of the trail kit and found it satisfactory — primarily because the biscuit supply is stored elsewhere and therefore irrelevant to any plan she may or may not have for the weekend.

Ellie: Watched Steve load the Jeep for eleven minutes with an expression of deep interest, then went inside and watched the Jeep through the window as though it might do something different. It did not. She remained interested anyway.

Jersey: Has identified the warmest available indoor position in advance of tomorrow's absence and has been occupying it since noon as a precautionary measure. He is already comfortable. He will be comfortable all weekend. This is the plan.
founding_editor_note: I built a workbench once for a man who spent a week measuring before he touched a board. He built the best thing he'd ever built. The measuring is not wasted time — it's how you know the first cut is right. Go find that ridgeline. — D.

in_brief.sport: Friday Strength Report: Christine lifted the week, lifted the quarter, and will apparently lift a sixty-three-year cartographic dispute off a ridgeline tomorrow — by going and standing on it.
in_brief.forecast: Saturday: clear at elevation · contested terrain · one survey team to be definitively proven wrong · the Jeep holds · bring the duck.

storyline_updates.title: The Third Fold
storyline_updates.summary: Eve-of-departure edition complete. Christine has closed the planning phase and confirmed the coordinates. Steve is at nine out of ten. The pencil mark in the survey margin has quietly settled which 1962 team was wrong, though no one has said so aloud. The Jeep is ready, Adam's recommendation has been absorbed, and Orvell's postcard sits beside the brass duck. Tomorrow they go. Day 4 resolves on Saturday: the ridgeline is reached, the dispute is settled by presence rather than paper, and whatever 'Disputed (Atmospheric)' actually means is found out at first hand. Resolution should be warm, funny, and true — Christine finds the thing, Steve has already quietly seen it, and the brass duck turns out to have been pointing somewhere worth pointing.
